title: Hieracium pseudoschenkii
taxonomy: Hieracium pseudoschenkii belongs to the family Asteraceae, commonly known as the daisy or sunflower family. It is a member of the genus Hieracium, which includes hawkweeds, a group of flowering plants. Within the genus Hieracium, Hieracium pseudoschenkii can be further classified as a species within the subgenus Pilosella. The species is often divided into subspecies and varieties, which helps to distinguish between different populations. Its classification is based on morphological characteristics, genetic studies, and other scientific criteria.

intro: Hieracium pseudoschenkii, also known as a species of hawkweed, is a flowering plant that belongs to the dandelion family (Asteraceae). It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial activities, potentially providing relief from various health issues such as digestive problems, skin irritations, and respiratory infections. The bioactive constituents of Hieracium pseudoschenkii include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and sesquiterpenoids, which are responsible for its therapeutic effects. In traditional medicine, Hieracium pseudoschenkii is often prepared as a tea, tincture, or infusion, making it a popular ingredient in herbal remedies for its potential health benefits.
